title: Go HTTP Upload and Download Gateways · Go Semantics Case: Context Goroutine Cleanup
summary: A Go handler buffers multipart forms and S3 objects in memory, trusts proxy headers, writes status too early, and continues transferring after the client leaves. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the context goroutine cleanup increment in streaming Go file-gateway handler. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Tie storage calls, progress work, pipes, and goroutines to request and shutdown contexts and prove termination.
Delay response commitment until safe, set representation headers, stream bounded data, and record partial-write terminal states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Context Goroutine Cleanup · Theory Context Goroutine Cleanup
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 5650 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate a streaming Go file handler on request admission and goroutine cleanup; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Context Goroutine Cleanup contract for the supplied data.
